mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer spending and real estate investment, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-occurring observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum and capacity utilization, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 45% of months where industrial production increases, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls create a restrictive monetary and weakening labor environment, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rates above 4% and falling payrolls yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where real GDP growth surpasses 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels increase expectations for aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 1.5 points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate coinciding with absolute declines in nonfarm payrolls signals severe labor market deterioration and recessionary pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of unemployment above 6% and falling payrolls fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances.
